name: public_safety_model_based_triage_tool_southport_st_lt
premise: Resource deployment, civil liberties, and downstream liability are tightening together. A unified command post is managing an active public-safety event with incomplete field reports and conflicting eyewitness accounts. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: public safety model based triage tool southport st. The moral tension is explicitly framed as short term versus long term: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. Telemetry and logs are diverging; the SOC lead will freeze writes in twelve minutes if no accountable owner signs off. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Zara Kim
agents.identity: I am Zara Kim, the accountable decision-maker on this Public Safety bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes long term whenever someone invokes short term.

agents.name: Hector Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Hector Vasquez, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so long term is not sacrificed by default just because short term feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect long term.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Zara Kim must decide under short term versus long term. What does Zara Kim do?
